DP.059.0000:
Welcome!
This is the Oreburgh Pokémon Gym!\f
I’m Roark, the Gym Leader!\f
I’m but one Trainer who decided to
walk proudly with Rock-type Pokémon!\f
As the Gym Leader, I need to see
your potential as a Trainer.\f
And, I’ll need to see the toughness
of the Pokémon that battle with you!\f
DP.059.0001:
This is embarrassing...\f
I went and lost to a Trainer who
didn’t have a single Gym Badge...\f
But that’s tough. You were strong,
and I was weak. That’s all there is.\f
According to Pokémon League rules,
I have to give you our Gym Badge\r
since you’ve beaten me, the Leader.\f
Here’s your official Pokémon League
Coal Badge.\f
DP.059.0002:
[0103,0000] received
the Coal Badge from Roark!
DP.059.0003:
Having that Coal Badge means your
Pokémon can now use the hidden move\r
Rock Smash outside of battle.\f
You should also take this, too.\f
DP.059.0004:
That [0108,0000] contains
the move Stealth Rock.\f
It’s a move that inflicts damage on
foes that switch into battle.\f
Incidentally, a TM will teach its move
to a Pokémon instantly.\f
But a TM will break when you use it,
so make certain you want a Pokémon\r
to learn that move.
DP.059.0005:
In this region of Sinnoh, there are
seven other Gym Leaders.\f
I should warn you--they’re a lot
tougher than I am!
DP.059.0006:
Howdy!
How’s it going, Champ-to-be?\f
That’s what I said to a really
impatient boy earlier, too.\f
The Gym Leader is a user of
Rock-type Pokémon.\f
Well, listen. Rock-type Pokémon
really hate water, all right?\f
They’re also weak to Grass-type
moves.\f
Gee, they sure have weaknesses!\f
But don’t think it will be easy.\f
You don’t get to be a Gym Leader
without covering for weaknesses.\f
Going after a Rock-type Pokémon
with a Fire-type Pokémon won’t be\r
easy, either.\f
That’s all the advice I can give.
Thanks for listening!
DP.059.0007:
Oh! [0103,0000]!
You’ve taken care of the Gym Leader!\f
So, did my advice come in handy
or what?\f
If my advice was useful, it’d be
great if you’d become my fan!

Leader: Roark\f
